tasks.title: Open your private workspace
tasks.summary: Start from the protected stack dashboard.

surfaces.now: Open the private workspace and confirm the stack is reachable.
surfaces.why: The first step is simply entering a protected place you own.
deeper: The dashboard is protected by Keycloak edge authentication.

reward.name: Workspace Entered
reward.capability: You reached the protected operating layer.

tasks.title: Open BookStack
tasks.summary: Use BookStack as the first owned service.

surfaces.now: Open BookStack and see that it is part of the stack.
surfaces.why: BookStack touches route, login, database, uploads, backup, restore, logs, and runbooks.
deeper: BookStack is selected as the first service because it is useful and operationally representative.

reward.name: First Service Seen
reward.capability: You identified one real service in the stack.

tasks.title: Protect BookStack with central login
tasks.summary: BookStack ownership starts with evidence that access is controlled.

surfaces.now: Verify BookStack route and Keycloak/OIDC access evidence.
surfaces.why: A service is not owned until access to it is intentional and proven.
deeper: The public root uses Keycloak/OIDC as the identity source.
Authenticated-user and wrong-group probes can be added by deployed browser tests.

reward.name: First Door Secured
reward.capability: You proved BookStack is behind central access control.

tasks.title: Map BookStack state
tasks.summary: Find the volume and database behind BookStack.

surfaces.now: Review BookStack volume and database mapping.
surfaces.why: You cannot recover what you cannot locate.
deeper: BookStack has application files in a Docker volume and relational state in MariaDB.

reward.name: State Mapped
reward.capability: You know where BookStack data lives.

tasks.title: Prove BookStack can be restored
tasks.summary: Backups only count when restore has been proven.

surfaces.now: Run or review a temporary restore drill for BookStack.
surfaces.why: Untested recovery is still theoretical ownership.
deeper: A full restore proof should use a temporary isolated environment.
The MVP command records safe drill preparation until service-specific restore automation is added.

reward.name: Restore Proven
reward.capability: You have proven this service can come back from backup.

tasks.title: Reveal BookStack commands
tasks.summary: See the command line behind the dashboard.

surfaces.now: Reveal the `stackctl` commands that inspect BookStack.
surfaces.why: The dashboard is scaffolding; the CLI is repeatable control.
deeper: zsh completions make service IDs and task IDs discoverable.

reward.name: Command Revealed
reward.capability: You can inspect the same state from the CLI.
